name: lessons-stop-session-scope
doc: Multi-session isolation for the fuse-lessons Stop reminder (the production bug: 2-3 concurrent Claude sessions on different projects, one session's Stop lists the WRONG project's LESSON.md and steals the reminder). Steps share HOME=$TMP, so the home-rooted session-roots registry ($HOME/.fuse-harness/cache/lessons/session-roots.json) is shared exactly as it is across real concurrent sessions. Two projects under $TMP each carry a package.json marker so projectRootOrNull resolves to distinct roots. Session s1 code-edits project A; session s2 code-edits project B (PostToolUse, scope lessons → post-tracking.ts markWrite, keyed by payload.session_id). Stop s2 must list ONLY project B (regex forbids 'projA', requires 'projB/MEMORY/LESSON.md'); Stop s1 must then STILL list project A — proving s2's Stop neither showed nor consumed s1's pending reminder. On the pre-fix global-registry code, Stop s2 would list AND throttle both roots, so Stop s1 would come back empty: the scoping regression is caught by step 4.

steps.doc: Session s1 edits a code file in project A → marks (s1, projA).editedAt. PostToolUse mark is side-effect only.
Session s2 edits a code file in project B → marks (s2, projB).editedAt, in the SAME shared registry.
Stop s2 → reminder lists ONLY project B; project A must NOT appear (no misdirection).
Stop s1 → still lists project A. Pre-fix (global registry) this would be empty because s2's Stop already consumed/throttled projA.
